seoTitle: How to Set Reminders on Monday.com (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Configure due-date reminders in Monday.com using automation recipes so your team gets timely alerts before deadlines hit.
intro: Missed deadlines often come down to no one getting reminded in time. This guide shows how to configure automatic due-date reminders in Monday.com, from setting up date columns to using the Automation Center's pre-built recipes for relative-time alerts sent by email, Slack, or internal notification.

steps.heading: Add a Date or Timeline column
steps.body: Configure the deadline column your reminders will reference on your master project board.

steps.heading: Open the Automation Center
steps.body: Browse pre-built "Notification" and "Due Date" recipes inside the Automation Center.

steps.heading: Set your trigger window
steps.body: Configure relative time triggers, such as sending a reminder one day before, two hours before, or on the exact due date.

steps.heading: Customize the notification message
steps.body: Personalize the message layout using item tokens like Task Name, Assigned Person, and Status fields.

steps.heading: Add conditional reminder rules
steps.body: Build rules so alerts only trigger if a task status is still "Working on it" or "Stuck," avoiding reminders for finished work.

steps.heading: Review the automation log
steps.body: Check the automation activity log to manage active recipes and confirm reminders are firing correctly.

faq.question: Can I set a reminder for a specific time before a deadline?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure relative time triggers to send reminders a set number of hours, days, or weeks before a due date.

faq.question: Will I get reminded about tasks that are already finished?
faq.answer: You can add conditional filters so reminders only fire for tasks still marked "Working on it" or "Stuck," skipping completed items.

faq.question: How are reminders delivered?
faq.answer: Reminders can be sent via email, Slack, or internal Monday.com notifications, depending on how you configure the recipe.

faq.question: Can I customize what the reminder message says?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize the message text using dynamic tokens like Task Name, Assigned Person, and Status.
